docs.title: ads-tec Industrial IT JSON-RPC API
docs.text: JSON-RPC 2.0 API for ads-tec Industrial IT devices over HTTPS. This documentation covers the full device API and provides a Python reference implementation.

docs.title: Supported Devices
docs.text: Device Family Models Firmware Version Firewalls IRF1000, IRF2000, IRF3000 IRF 2.2.8 Web Terminals All ads-tec Web terminals AWT 14.2.8

docs.title: API Capabilities
docs.text:
• Authentication — Session-based login with SID management
  • Configuration — Read and write device configuration variables with transactional sessions
  • Status Monitoring — Query device status properties (firmware version, CPU stats, network state, etc.)
  • Table Operations — CRUD operations on device tables (users, routing, etc.)
  • File Transfer — Upload firmware, certificates, settings; download diagnostics and backups
  • GPIO Control — Read digital inputs and control LEDs (firewall models)
  • Password Strength — Validate passwords using the built-in zxcvbn algorithm

docs.title: Quick Example (Python Reference Implementation)
docs.text:
import jsonrpcdevice

# Connect to device
dev = jsonrpcdevice.AdstecJSONRPCDevice("192.168.0.254", "admin", "admin")

# Query status
version = dev.status("imageversion")
print(f"Firmware: {version}")

# Change configuration
dev.config_set_commit({"ntp_service": "enabled", "ntp_server": "pool.ntp.org"})

# Clean up
dev.logout()

docs.title: Documentation Structure
docs.text:
The documentation is organized into three sections by device category:

  • All Devices — API reference and guides that apply to every ads-tec Industrial IT device
  • Firewalls (IRF1000 / IRF2000 / IRF3000) — GPIO, IPsec, OpenVPN, CUT & ALARM, 3G/4G, GNSS
  • Web Terminals — Web Panel display and browser configuration

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Getting Started — Installation and first connection
  • JSON-RPC Methods — Core API method reference (all devices)
  • JSON Schemas — Machine-readable request/response schemas
  • Python Reference Implementation — Python client documentation
  • Firewall Methods — GPIO and IPsec (firewalls only)
  • Web Terminal Config — Web Panel (terminals only)

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.6+
  • Network access to an ads-tec device
  • Valid device credentials (username and password)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Clone the repository:

git clone https://github.com/ads-tec/Python-AdstecJSONRPCDevice.git
cd Python-AdstecJSONRPCDevice

Install the required dependency:

pip install requests

docs.title: First Connection
docs.text:
import jsonrpcdevice

# Create a device instance
dev = jsonrpcdevice.AdstecJSONRPCDevice(
    target="192.168.0.254",   # Device IP address
    user="admin",             # Username
    pw="admin",               # Password
    timeout=120.0,            # Request timeout in seconds (optional)
    verify=False              # SSL certificate verification (optional)
)

# Query the firmware version
version = dev.status("imageversion")
print(f"Firmware version: {version}")

# Always log out when done
dev.logout()

SSL Verification

By default, SSL certificate verification is disabled (verify=False) because ads-tec devices typically use self-signed certificates. Set verify=True if your device has a trusted certificate installed.

Automatic Authentication

You do not need to call get_sid() manually. The library automatically authenticates on the first API call.

docs.title: Understanding Configuration Sessions
docs.text: Changing device configuration requires a configuration session. This ensures that multiple changes are applied atomically.

docs.title: Manual Session Management
docs.text:
# 1. Start a session
cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()

# 2. Set values within the session
dev.config_set(cfg_session_id, {
    "ntp_service": "enabled",
    "ntp_server": "pool.ntp.org"
})

# 3. Commit the session to apply changes
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)

docs.title: Convenience Method
docs.text:
For simple changes, use config_set_commit() which handles session management automatically:

dev.config_set_commit({
    "ntp_service": "enabled",
    "ntp_server": "pool.ntp.org"
})

docs.title: Persisting Changes with save_now
docs.text:
By default (nvram_mode = "commit"), config_set_commit() applies changes immediately but does not save them to persistent storage (NVRAM). Changes that are only committed will be lost on the next power cycle.

To save committed changes permanently, set the save_now trigger variable:

dev.config_set_commit({"save_now": "1"})

Test before you save

It is strongly recommended to verify that the device is still reachable after applying changes — especially for network settings on remote devices — before persisting with save_now. If a misconfiguration makes the device unreachable, an on-site power cycle will restore the previous working configuration.

# 1. Apply new settings (not yet persistent)
dev.config_set_commit({"lan_gateway": "10.0.0.1"})

# 2. Verify the device is still reachable
online, _ = jsonrpcdevice.check_host("192.168.0.254", timeout=5)
if online:
    # 3. Only now persist to NVRAM
    dev.config_set_commit({"save_now": "1"})
    print("Settings saved permanently")
else:
    print("Device unreachable — power cycle to restore previous config")

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
try:
    dev = jsonrpcdevice.AdstecJSONRPCDevice("192.168.0.254", "admin", "wrong_password")
    dev.get_sid()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Login failed: {e}")

  • Python Reference Implementation — All methods and parameters

  • Status Properties — Available status queries

docs.title: Configuration Variables
docs.text: Configuration variables are read with config.get() and written with config.set() within a configuration session.

docs.title: Reading Configuration
docs.text: result = dev.config_get(["lan_ipaddr", "timezone", "ntp_service"])

docs.title: Writing Configuration
docs.text: dev.config_set_commit({"timezone": "Europe/Berlin", "ntp_service": "enabled"})

docs.title: Variable Reference
docs.text:
For a complete list of all configuration variables with defaults, allowed values, and validation rules, see the per-product Configuration Database reference:

Product Reference AWT1000 (Web Terminal) AWT1000 Configuration Database IRF1401 IRF1401 Configuration Database IRF1421 IRF1421 Configuration Database IRF3401 IRF3401 Configuration Database IRF3421 IRF3421 Configuration Database IRF3801 IRF3801 Configuration Database IRF3821 IRF3821 Configuration Database

See also the Configuration Database Overview for a comparison of features across products.

docs.title: JSON-RPC Methods
docs.text: This page documents the JSON-RPC 2.0 methods available on ads-tec Industrial IT devices. The Python reference implementation wraps these methods for convenience, but any HTTP client that can send JSON-RPC requests can use this API.

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text: All requests are sent as POST https://<device-ip>/rpc with Content-Type: application/json.

docs.title: Request Format
docs.text:
{
    "id": "req-1",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "method": "call",
    "params": ["<session_id>", "<object>", "<method>", {<parameters>}]
}

Use "method": "list" instead of "call" to get a description of an object and its available methods.

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
{
    "id": "req-1",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": [0, {<result_data>}]
}

The first element of result is the return code (0 = success). The second element contains the response data.

docs.title: Error Response
docs.text:
{
    "id": "req-1",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "error": {"code": -32000, "message": "error description"}
}

docs.title: Available Objects
docs.text: Object Description session Authentication and session management config Configuration read/write and table operations status Device status queries gpio LEDs, buttons, digital I/O — firewalls only network.ipsec.control IPsec VPN tunnel control — firewalls only statusd Big-LinX VPN control blxpush Big-LinX IIoT dashboard data push file Base64 file upload/download via JSON-RPC datacollection Real-time traffic statistics and time-series data zxcvbn Password strength checking

docs.title: session — Authentication
docs.text:
Method Parameters Description create user, password, timeout (optional) Authenticate and get a session ID list — List all active sessions destroy sid Destroy a session (logout)

session.create is the only call that may use the null SID 00000000000000000000000000000000.

Parameters for create:

Parameter Type Required Description user string yes Username password string yes Password timeout integer no Session timeout in seconds (default: 600)

Response: Returns sid (32-char hex string) and acls (access rights for this user).

Python:

dev.get_sid()    # session.create
dev.logout()     # session.destroy

docs.title: Session Management
docs.text:
Method Parameters Description sess_start — Start a configuration session. Returns cfg_session_id sess_commit cfg_session_id Commit and apply changes atomically sess_abort cfg_session_id Cancel all changes in the session

All-or-Nothing

On conflict (e.g., invalid combination of settings), sess_commit discards the entire config set and returns an error.

docs.title: Read Operations
docs.text: Method Parameters Description get keys (list) Read configuration variable values. Non-existing variables return empty string get_values keys (list) Extended read: returns default value, permissions, JSON-interpreted value, and raw string get_default keys (list) Get default value for each variable (firmware/product-specific) export_pages pages (list) Export all settings for given page IDs. Output can be fed to import_config

docs.title: Write Operations
Table Operations
docs.text:
Method Parameters Description table_get tablename, condition (dict, max 2 keys) Read matching rows table_set tablename, cfg_session_id, row (array) Insert a new row (all columns required). Integer id columns require an explicit value — there is no auto-increment table_up tablename, cfg_session_id, condition (dict), values (dict) Update matching rows table_del tablename, cfg_session_id, condition (dict) Delete matching rows

See Tables for known table schemas.

Python:

dev.sess_start()                          # config.sess_start
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)           # config.sess_commit
dev.config_get(["lan_ipaddr", "timezone"])  # config.get
dev.config_set(cfg_session_id, values)    # config.set
dev.config_set_commit(values)             # sess_start + set + sess_commit

docs.title: status — Device Status
docs.text:
Method Parameters Description get function, parameters (list, optional) Query a status property

Python:

dev.status("imageversion")              # No parameters
dev.status("ping4", "127.0.0.1", "3")  # With parameters

See Status Properties for the full list.

docs.title: Firewall-Only Methods
docs.text: The gpio and network.ipsec.control objects are available on firewall models (IRF1000, IRF2000, IRF3000) only. See JSON-RPC Methods (Firewalls).

docs.title: statusd — Big-LinX VPN Control
docs.text: Method Parameters Description blx_status — Full Big-LinX diagnostic status blx_vpn_up — Start Big-LinX VPN connection vpn_down — Stop Big-LinX VPN connection

docs.title: blx_status Response Fields
docs.text:
Field Type Description cardstate string Smart card state. "VPNSC_CS_READY" = VPN can be activated tokenlabel string Common Name of Big-LinX X.509 certificate vpn_state_name string Current OpenVPN state (see Big-LinX status values) vpn_ip string VPN interface IP (empty if down) vpn_server_ip string VPN server public IP and port vpn_ctrl_state integer Target state: 1 = should be up, 0 = should be down

Python:

blx = dev.call("statusd", "blx_status")
dev.call("statusd", "blx_vpn_up")
dev.call("statusd", "vpn_down")

docs.title: blxpush — Big-LinX IIoT Data Push
docs.text:
Push measurement data to the Big-LinX IIoT dashboard.

Note

Requires Big-LinX connection configured and Big-LinX IoT feature enabled.

Method Parameters Description status — Connection state and buffer status push measurement, tags, values Push data to Big-LinX database

docs.title: push Parameters
docs.text:
Parameter Type Description measurement string Measurement name in the Big-LinX database tags array Additional tags for the data values array of objects Key-value pairs of integer/float data. Must include "ts" key with millisecond-resolution timestamp

Python:

status = dev.call("blxpush", "status")
dev.call("blxpush", "push",
    measurement="temperature",
    tags=["sensor1"],
    values=[{"ts": 1708099200000, "temp_celsius": 23.5}]
)

docs.title: file — Base64 File Transfer
docs.text:
Upload and download files via JSON-RPC using Base64 encoding. Alternative to the HTTP upload/download endpoints.

Method Parameters Description write path, data Upload a Base64-encoded file read path Download a file as Base64 delete path Delete a file to free RAM

docs.title: Allowed Paths
docs.text:
Path Purpose /tmp/upsettigs Settings (.cf2) upload /tmp/upcerts Certificate upload /tmp/root/settings.cf2 Settings download (read/delete only)

Python:

import base64

# Upload a certificate via JSON-RPC
with open("cert.pem", "rb") as f:
    data = base64.b64encode(f.read()).decode()
dev.call("file", "write", path="/tmp/upcerts", data=data)

# Download settings via JSON-RPC
result = dev.call("file", "read", path="/tmp/root/settings.cf2")
# result["data"] contains Base64-encoded content

docs.title: datacollection — Traffic & System Statistics
docs.text:
The datacollection object provides real-time time-series data for network interface traffic (bytes, packets) and system resource usage (CPU, memory, load, uptime) on all devices. On firewalls (IRF), it additionally includes packet filter byte counters.

Data is collected every 10 seconds and stored in a ring buffer with 1-second resolution, retaining approximately the last 10 minutes of data.

docs.title: Metric Names
docs.text:
Metric names follow a dot-separated naming convention. Use get_metrics to discover all available metrics on a device.

Network traffic (all devices):

Pattern Example Description {interface}.rx_bytes ETH1.rx_bytes Received bytes (delta per interval) {interface}.tx_bytes ETH1.tx_bytes Transmitted bytes (delta per interval) {interface}.rx_packets ETH1.rx_packets Received packets (delta per interval) {interface}.tx_packets ETH1.tx_packets Transmitted packets (delta per interval)

System resources (all devices, since firmware 2.2.8):

Pattern Example Description system.cpu system.cpu Aggregate CPU usage across all cores (percent) system.cpu.{N} system.cpu.0 Per-core CPU usage (percent), numbered from 0 system.memory.used system.memory.used Used memory (bytes) system.memory.free system.memory.free Free memory (bytes) system.memory.total system.memory.total Total memory (bytes) system.load.1 system.load.1 1-minute load average system.load.5 system.load.5 5-minute load average system.load.15 system.load.15 15-minute load average system.uptime system.uptime System uptime (seconds)

Packet filter counters (firewalls only):

Pattern Example Description filter.rule.{chain}.{target}.{index}.bcnt filter.rule.INPUT.DROP.10.bcnt Byte count delta for a specific filter rule filter.policy.{chain}.{action}.bcnt filter.policy.INPUT.ACCEPT.bcnt Byte count delta for a chain's default policy

Network traffic and packet filter values represent deltas per collection interval (typically 10 seconds), not absolute counters. System resource metrics are point-in-time gauge values (the instantaneous reading at each collection interval).

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Parameters Description get_metrics — List all known metric names get_values_as_table metrics, from, to, resolution Query time-series data grouped by metric

docs.title: get_metrics
docs.text:
List all known metric names on the device.

result = dev.call("datacollection", "get_metrics")
metrics = result["result"]
# ["ETH1.rx_bytes", "ETH1.tx_bytes", "system.cpu", "system.cpu.0",
#  "system.memory.used", "system.load.1", "system.uptime",
#  "filter.rule.INPUT.DROP.10.bcnt", ...]

docs.title: get_values_as_table
docs.text:
Query time-series data grouped by metric with timestamps.

Parameter Type Description metrics array of string Metric names to query from integer Start time (Unix timestamp) to integer End time (Unix timestamp) resolution integer Buffer resolution in seconds (use 1) 
import time

now = int(time.time())
raw = dev.call("datacollection", "get_values_as_table",
    metrics=["ETH1.rx_bytes", "ETH1.tx_bytes"],
    **{"from": now - 600, "to": now, "resolution": 1})
data = raw["result"]
# {"ETH1.rx_bytes": [{"ts": 1771510200, "val": 3323.0}, ...],
#  "ETH1.tx_bytes": [{"ts": 1771510200, "val": 240.0}, ...]}

Each metric key maps to an array of {"ts": <unix_timestamp>, "val": <value>} objects. val is null when no data was collected for that time slot.

from is a Python keyword

Use **{"from": ...} syntax since from is a reserved word in Python.

docs.title: zxcvbn — Password Strength
docs.text:
Method Parameters Description get password Check password strength, returns entropy score

Threshold: entropy >= 75.0 = strong password.

Python:

result = dev.call("zxcvbn", "get", password="mypassword")
entropy = result["entropy"]

docs.title: Raw Call Example
docs.text:
For any method, use dev.call() directly:

# dev.call(object, method, **params)
result = dev.call("gpio", "get_bool", signal="DI2")

This sends:

{
    "id": "req-1",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "method": "call",
    "params": ["<sid>", "gpio", "get_bool", {"signal": "DI2"}]
}

docs.title: jsonrpcdevice.check_host(host, timeout=5)
docs.text:
Check if a host is online by making an HTTPS request.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default host

Hostname or IP address.

required timeout

Request timeout in seconds (default: 5).

5

Returns:

Type Description

A tuple (is_online, info). is_online: Boolean indicating if the host is reachable. info: Status code if online, or error message if offline.

Time to wait between checks in seconds (default: 3).

3

Returns:

Type Description

An instance of AdstecJSONRPCDevice once the device is ready.

300 interval

docs.title: JSON Schemas
docs.text: Machine-readable JSON Schema files are available in the schemas/ directory of the repository. These can be used for request validation, code generation, or integration with other tools.

docs.title: Available Schemas
docs.text: File Description jsonrpc-envelope.schema.json Base JSON-RPC 2.0 request/response/error envelope session.schema.json session.create, session.list, session.destroy config.schema.json config.get, config.get_values, config.get_default, config.set, config.export_pages, config.import_config, session management, table operations status.schema.json status.get with all known property names gpio.schema.json gpio.list, gpio.get, gpio.get_bool, gpio.get_pulses, gpio.on, gpio.off (firewalls only) ipsec.schema.json network.ipsec.control.up, network.ipsec.control.down (firewalls only) statusd.schema.json statusd.blx_status, statusd.blx_vpn_up, statusd.vpn_down blxpush.schema.json blxpush.status, blxpush.push file-rpc.schema.json file.write, file.read, file.delete zxcvbn.schema.json zxcvbn.get file-transfer.schema.json HTTP upload/download endpoints (not JSON-RPC)

docs.title: Request Envelope
docs.text:
All JSON-RPC calls use the same envelope. The top-level method is always "call" (or "list" to discover methods on an object), with params as a 4-element array:

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": [
    "<session_id>",
    "<object>",
    "<method>",
    { <method_parameters> }
  ]
}

Available objects: session, config, status, gpio, network.ipsec.control, statusd, blxpush, file, zxcvbn.

Successful responses return result as [0, {data}]. Non-zero first element indicates an error.

SuccessError 
{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "result": [0, {"imageversion": "2.2.6"}]
}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "error": {"code": -32000, "message": "error description"}
}

docs.title: session.create
docs.text:
Authenticate and obtain a session ID. This is the only call that may use the null SID.

RequestResponse 
{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": [
    "00000000000000000000000000000000",
    "session", "create",
    {"user": "admin", "password": "admin", "timeout": 600}
  ]
}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "result": [0, {"sid": "a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4", "acls": {}}]
}
Parameter Type Required Description user string yes Login username password string yes Login password timeout integer no Session timeout in seconds (default: 600)

Returns sid (32-char hex) and acls (access rights for this user).

docs.title: session.list
docs.text:
List all active sessions on the device.

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "session", "list", {}]}

docs.title: session.destroy
docs.text:
Invalidate the current session (logout).

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "session", "destroy", {}]}

docs.title: config.sess_start
docs.text:
Start a configuration session. Returns cfg_session_id.

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "config", "sess_start", {}]}

docs.title: config.sess_commit
docs.text:
Commit all changes in the session atomically. On conflict, the entire config set is discarded.

Parameter Type Required Description cfg_session_id string yes Session ID from sess_start

docs.title: config.sess_abort
docs.text:
Cancel a configuration session and discard all pending changes.

Parameter Type Required Description cfg_session_id string yes Session ID from sess_start

docs.title: config.get
docs.text:
Read configuration variables. Non-existing variables return empty string.

RequestResponse 
{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": ["<sid>", "config", "get",
    {"keys": ["lan_ipaddr", "timezone"]}]
}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "result": [0, {"result": [
    {"lan_ipaddr": "192.168.0.254"},
    {"timezone": "Europe/Berlin"}
  ]}]
}
Parameter Type Required Description keys string[] yes Configuration variable names

docs.title: config.get_values
docs.text:
Extended read: returns default value, permissions, JSON-interpreted value, and raw string for each variable.

Parameter Type Required Description keys string[] yes Configuration variable names

docs.title: config.get_default
docs.text:
Get the firmware/product-specific default value for each variable.

Parameter Type Required Description keys string[] yes Configuration variable names

docs.title: config.export_pages
docs.text:
Export all settings for given page IDs. Output can be fed to import_config.

Parameter Type Required Description pages string[] yes Page IDs to export

docs.title: config.set
docs.text:
Write configuration variables (requires active session). Values are regex-validated; on failure the entire call is aborted.

Parameter Type Required Description cfg_session_id string yes Active session ID values object yes Key-value pairs to set verbose boolean no Return all validation errors instead of just the first (default: true)

docs.title: config.import_config
docs.text:
Bulk import configuration. Does not require a config session. All-or-nothing — aborts on any error.

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": ["<sid>", "config", "import_config", {
    "jsondata": {
      "configdata": {"timezone": "Europe/Berlin"},
      "tableinsert": {},
      "tableupdate": {},
      "tabledelete": {}
    }
  }]
}
Parameter Type Required Description jsondata object yes Import payload with configdata, tableinsert, tableupdate, tabledelete

docs.title: config.table_get
docs.text:
Read table rows matching a condition (max 2 keys, AND logic).

Parameter Type Required Description tablename string yes Table name (e.g. "users", "ipgroups", "macgroups", "permissions") condition object yes Match condition (max 2 keys)

docs.title: config.table_set
docs.text:
Insert a new row (requires active session). All columns required.

Parameter Type Required Description tablename string yes Table name cfg_session_id string yes Active session ID row string[] yes Ordered array of field values

docs.title: config.table_up
docs.text:
Update matching rows (requires active session).

Parameter Type Required Description tablename string yes Table name cfg_session_id string yes Active session ID condition object yes Match condition (max 2 keys) values object yes Fields to update

docs.title: config.table_del
docs.text:
Delete matching rows (requires active session).

Parameter Type Required Description tablename string yes Table name cfg_session_id string yes Active session ID condition object yes Match condition (max 2 keys)

docs.title: status.get
docs.text:
Query a status property.

Simple (no params)With parametersInterface query 
{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": ["<sid>", "status", "get",
    {"function": "imageversion", "parameters": ["", ""]}]
}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": ["<sid>", "status", "get",
    {"function": "ping4", "parameters": ["127.0.0.1", "3"]}]
}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": ["<sid>", "status", "get",
    {"function": "if_status", "parameters": ["eth0", ""]}]
}
Parameter Type Required Description function string yes Status property name parameters string[] no Up to 2 optional parameters (default: ["", ""])

docs.title: gpio — GPIO Control (Firewalls Only)
docs.text:
Note

Available on firewall models (IRF1000, IRF2000, IRF3000) only. See JSON-RPC Methods (Firewalls).

All GPIO methods use dev.call("gpio", "<method>", signal="<name>").

Method Parameters Description list — List all GPIO signals get signal Get signal value get_bool signal Get boolean state get_pulses signal Get pulse count (rising + falling edges; 1 button press = 2) on signal Activate signal off signal Deactivate signal

docs.title: IPsec VPN (Firewalls Only)
docs.text:
Note

Available on firewall models (IRF1000, IRF2000, IRF3000) only. The IPsec policy must be configured as "Active (Switched)" before it can be controlled via API. See JSON-RPC Methods (Firewalls).

Method Parameters Description up policy identifier Activate an IPsec policy down policy identifier Deactivate an IPsec policy 
{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "network.ipsec.control", "up", {}]}

docs.title: statusd.blx_status
docs.text:
Full Big-LinX diagnostic status.

RequestResponse 
{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "statusd", "blx_status", {}]}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "result": [0, {
    "cardstate": "VPNSC_CS_READY",
    "tokenlabel": "device-001.biglinx.cloud",
    "vpn_state_name": "CONNECTED",
    "vpn_ip": "10.8.0.5",
    "vpn_server_ip": "203.0.113.1:1194",
    "vpn_ctrl_state": 1
  }]
}
Field Type Description cardstate string Smart card state. "VPNSC_CS_READY" = VPN can be activated tokenlabel string Common Name of Big-LinX X.509 certificate vpn_state_name string Current OpenVPN state vpn_ip string VPN interface IP (empty if down) vpn_server_ip string VPN server public IP and port vpn_ctrl_state integer Target state: 1 = should be up, 0 = should be down

docs.title: statusd.blx_vpn_up / statusd.vpn_down
docs.text:
Start or stop the Big-LinX VPN connection.

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "statusd", "blx_vpn_up", {}]}

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "statusd", "vpn_down", {}]}

docs.title: Big-LinX IIoT Data Push
docs.text:
Note

Requires Big-LinX connection configured and Big-LinX IoT feature enabled.

docs.title: blxpush.status
docs.text:
Get connection state and buffer status.

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "blxpush", "status", {}]}

docs.title: blxpush.push
docs.text:
Push measurement data to the Big-LinX database.

Request 
{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "call",
  "params": ["<sid>", "blxpush", "push", {
    "measurement": "temperature",
    "tags": ["sensor1"],
    "values": [{"ts": 1708099200000, "temp_celsius": 23.5}]
  }]
}
Parameter Type Required Description measurement string yes Measurement name in the Big-LinX database tags string[] yes Additional tags for the data values object[] yes Data points. Each must include ts (millisecond timestamp) plus key-value pairs of integer/float data

docs.title: Base64 File Transfer (JSON-RPC)
docs.text: Upload and download files via JSON-RPC using Base64 encoding. Alternative to the HTTP file transfer endpoints.

docs.title: file.write
docs.text:
Upload a Base64-encoded file.

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "file", "write",
   {"path": "/tmp/upcerts", "data": "<base64-encoded-content>"}]}

docs.title: file.read
docs.text:
Download a file as Base64.

RequestResponse 
{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "file", "read",
   {"path": "/tmp/root/settings.cf2"}]}

{
  "id": "req-1",
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "result": [0, {"data": "<base64-encoded-content>"}]
}

docs.title: file.delete
docs.text:
Delete a file to free RAM.

{"id": "req-1", "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "call",
 "params": ["<sid>", "file", "delete",
   {"path": "/tmp/root/settings.cf2"}]}

docs.title: Allowed Paths
docs.text: Path Operations Purpose /tmp/upsettigs write Settings (.cf2) upload /tmp/upcerts write Certificate upload /tmp/root/settings.cf2 read, delete Settings download

docs.title: zxcvbn.get
File Transfer (HTTP)
docs.text: File upload and download use HTTP endpoints, not JSON-RPC. Authentication is via the ads_sid cookie (session ID from session.create).

docs.title: Upload
docs.text:
POST https://<device-ip>/priv/script/php_rpc/upload.php

  • Content-Type: multipart/form-data
  • Cookie: ads_sid=<session_id>
  • Form field name = file type, value = file binary
File Type Description firmware Firmware binary — triggers reboot bootlogo Custom boot logo settings Settings backup (.cf2) customer_settings Customer-specific settings cert Certificate (CA, server, client PEM) wwan_update WWAN modem firmware

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
GET https://<device-ip>/priv/script/php_rpc/download.php?file=<filename>

  • Cookie: ads_sid=<session_id>
Filename Prerequisite Description diag.tar.gz Set generate_diag_now = "1" first Diagnostic archive settings.cf2 Set save_settings_now = "1" first Settings backup

docs.title: Status Properties
docs.text: Status properties are queried with status.get(). They are read-only and return current device state.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Simple query (no parameters)
version = dev.status("imageversion")

# Query with parameters
ping_result = dev.status("ping4", "127.0.0.1", "3")

docs.title: System Information
docs.text: Property Parameters Returns Description system_name — string Device system name imageversion — string Current firmware version (e.g., "Ads-tec/IRF2xxx/2.6.5/SVN-R13781.B-69636") second_imageversion — string Firmware version on second partition firmwareupdate_prepared — "yes" / "no" Whether second partition has a valid firmware ready to activate firmwareupdate_progress — -1 or 0–100 Flashing percent complete, or -1 if no flashing is active firmwareupdate_inprogress — "yes" / "no" Whether a firmware update is currently in progress firmware_version_downgrade_check version string "yes" / "no" Whether a downgrade to the given version is possible (e.g., dev.status("firmware_version_downgrade_check", "1.5.0")) product — string Device family identifier (e.g., "IRF2xxx") — used for firmware file matching version — string Running firmware main version (e.g., "1.5.0") buildno — string Detailed revision and build number configstate — string Configuration state (e.g., "notsaved" if unsaved changes exist) redbootserial — string Device serial number redbootproduct — string Hardware revision / product identifier realproduct — string Product designation (e.g., "IF1110") product_alias — string Product alias name versionbuild — string Firmware version with build number (e.g., "2.0.6 (Build 55290)") boot_finished — "yes" / "no" Whether the device has completed its boot process uptime — string Time since last reboot + load average uptimesec — string Uptime in seconds cpustat — string CPU load in percent memstat — string Main memory utilization in percent meminfo process name string Memory utilization of a specified process simstate — "no sim" / "synced" / "not synced" Memory card state diag_log category string string Internal diagnostic log for a subsystem (e.g., dev.status("diag_log", "fw_update") for firmware update errors). Format may change between versions eventlog lines (int), filter (regex) multiline string Event log output. lines limits output (0 = all); filter is an extended regex (grep -E). Both optional reboot_timer — seconds Time remaining until scheduled reboot customersettings_size — number (kB) Size of customer settings customersettings_timestamp — unix timestamp Change date of customer settings

docs.title: Network Interfaces
docs.text:
Property Parameters Returns Description interfaces — string Space-separated list of Linux interface names currently available on the device based on its configuration. Interfaces appear here even if they are down — e.g. wwan1 appears when mobile is configured but not connected, ipsec0 appears when IPsec is configured. Example: "br1 ETH2 ETH3 ETH4 ETH5 ETH6 ETH7 lxcbr0 l3tap10 wwan1" symbolic_ifnames_with_umts_with_ipsec — string Space-separated list of symbolic interface names (e.g., "wan lan_port1 lan_port2 docker vpn10 umts"). Same interfaces as interfaces but using the symbolic names used internally for {name}_name config variable lookups if_status interface name string Physical interface status. Returns negotiated speed/duplex or "no link" if_mac interface name MAC address Hardware address (e.g., "00:CC:90:00:71:01") if_ip interface name IP address Interface IP address if_rxb interface name number Bytes received via interface if_txb interface name number Bytes sent via interface

Valid interface names for if_status, if_mac, if_ip, if_rxb, if_txb:

  • Physical: eth0, eth1, ETH1–ETH8 (IRF3000)
  • Bridge: br0, br1
  • VPN: l3tap0–l3tap10, ipsec0
  • Mobile: wwan1
  • Docker: lxcbr0

Resolve interface names dynamically

Each symbolic interface name (e.g. lan, wan) maps to a Linux name via the {name}_ifname config variable. Use config_get(["lan_ifname"]) to resolve the name, then query if_ip and if_mac. See Interface Names for the full mapping and examples/lan_ip_mac_status.py for a complete example.

docs.title: Network Diagnostics
docs.text: Property Parameters Returns Description ping4 host, count string IPv4 ping. Example: dev.status("ping4", "8.8.8.8", "3") traceroute4 host string Traceroute to host nslookup4 hostname string DNS lookup arping host "up" / "down" ARP-level host check tcpping host, port "Host is up" / "Host is down" TCP SYN check on a port routes — string System routing table nameserver — string Space-separated list of active DNS servers dhcp_gateway — IP address Current DHCP default gateway

docs.title: Date & Time
docs.text: Property Parameters Returns Description date — string Current system time and timezone (e.g., "Fri Oct 26 00:00:00 CEST 2012") timezone_list — string Space-separated list of available timezones ntpstat_sources — string NTP sync sources (newline-separated CSV). * in second field = active source

docs.title: NTP Source Parsing
docs.text:
ntpstat = dev.status("ntpstat_sources")
for line in ntpstat.split("\n"):
    fields = line.split(",")
    if fields[1] == "*":
        print(f"Synced to: {fields[2]}")

docs.title: Event Log
docs.text: The eventlog status property returns the device's syslog. It reads from file-based syslog if enabled, from SD card if available, or from the kernel ring buffer as fallback.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Parameter Position Type Description lines 1st integer as string Number of most recent lines to return. "0" = all lines filter 2nd string Extended regex pattern (grep -E) to filter log lines. Empty = no filter

Both parameters are optional. When omitted, the full unfiltered log is returned.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
# Get the last 10 log lines
log = dev.status("eventlog", "10")
print(log)

# Get all log lines (can be large)
full_log = dev.status("eventlog")

docs.title: Filtering with Regex
docs.text:
The filter parameter uses extended regular expressions (grep -E), supporting | for alternation, () for grouping, and other ERE syntax:

# Authentication events
auth_log = dev.status("eventlog", "20", "login|auth|session")

# Kernel messages
kern_log = dev.status("eventlog", "50", "kernel:")

# Network-related events
net_log = dev.status("eventlog", "0", "eth[0-9]|br[0-9]|link")

docs.title: Audit Events
docs.text:
The Linux audit subsystem (audispd) logs security-relevant events such as login attempts, configuration changes, and process executions. Filter for audispd to retrieve these:

audit_log = dev.status("eventlog", "100", "audispd")
for line in audit_log.strip().split("\n"):
    if line:
        print(line)

docs.title: Firewall-Only Properties
docs.text: OpenVPN, CUT & ALARM, 3G/4G UMTS/LTE, and GNSS/GPS status properties are available on firewall models only. See Status Properties (Firewalls).

docs.title: Big-LinX
docs.text: Property Parameters Returns Description blxstat_vpnstate — string Big-LinX VPN state (see values below) blxstat — JSON object Full Big-LinX diagnostic status (see fields below) wwh_status — "ERROR" / "ONLINE" / "OFFLINE" Big-LinX WWH connection status pintries — number PIN attempts remaining before smart card is blocked

docs.title: blxstat_vpnstate Values
docs.text: State Description "" (empty) Not running "OFF" VPN off "INITIAL" Initializing "CONNECTING" Connecting "ASSIGN_IP" Assigning IP "ADD_ROUTES" Adding routes "CONNECTED" Tunnel established "RECONNECTING" Reconnecting "EXITING" Shutting down "WAIT" Waiting "AUTH" Authenticating "GET_CONFIG" Retrieving config "RESOLVE" Resolving hostname "TCP_CONNECT" TCP connecting "WAITINGFORAPPROVAL" Pending approval (4-eyes principle)

docs.title: blxstat JSON Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description cardstate string Smart card state machine state tokenlabel string Common Name of Big-LinX X.509 VPN certificate vpn_state_name string Current VPN process state vpn_oldstate_name string Previous VPN state vpn_ip string VPN interface IP (empty if down) vpn_server_ip string VPN server public IP and port vpn_permanent string Whether connection is permanent vpn_ctrl_state integer Target VPN state: 1 = should be up, 0 = should be down wwh_lastbeat string Last WWH heartbeat wwh_service string WWH service state wwh_delay string WWH delay wwh_error string WWH error info

docs.title: Certificates
docs.text: Property Parameters Returns Description cacerts — string List of installed CA certificates clcerts — string List of installed client/server certificates print_cacert filename string Details of a CA certificate print_cert filename string Details of a client/server certificate

docs.title: SCEP
docs.text: Property Parameters Returns Description scep — "0" - "99" SCEP state machine: 0 = not activated; 10 = starting; 20 = waiting for CA certs; 40 = waiting for client certs; 50 = success; 60 = error

docs.title: Tables
docs.text: Some device data is stored in tables rather than flat configuration variables. Use the table operations to read, insert, update, and delete rows.

docs.title: Read a Row
docs.text: row = dev.table_get("users", "username", "admin")

docs.title: Insert a Row
docs.text:
cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("users", cfg_session_id, ["newuser", "", "", "1", "", "1970-01-01", "1970-01-01"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)

Integer id columns require explicit values

Tables with an integer id primary key (e.g. services, serv_Protocols, selected_services) do not support auto-increment. Passing "" or "0" for the id field causes a "datatype mismatch" error at commit. Always provide a unique non-zero integer.

Custom entries must use IDs starting at 1000 or higher. IDs below 1000 are reserved for factory defaults. Rules inserted with lower IDs may not appear in the device web UI and can conflict with firmware updates that add new factory defaults.

Alternatively, use config.import_config with tableinsert which uses the same dict format as export_pages output — but the same id rule applies.

docs.title: Update a Row
docs.text:
cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_up("users", cfg_session_id,
    condition={"username": "testuser"},
    values={"enabled": "1"}
)
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)

docs.title: Delete a Row
docs.text:
cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_del("users", cfg_session_id,
    condition={"username": "testuser"}
)
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)

docs.title: Query Semantics
docs.text:
• condition supports up to 2 key-value pairs (AND logic)
  • Example: {"username": "guest", "enabled": "0"} matches rows where both conditions are true
  • Non-existing variables return empty string, not an error

docs.title: Table Reference
docs.text:
For a complete list of all tables with column definitions, validation rules, and factory defaults, see the per-product Configuration Database reference:

Product Reference AWT1000 (Web Terminal) AWT1000 Configuration Database IRF1401 IRF1401 Configuration Database IRF1421 IRF1421 Configuration Database IRF3401 IRF3401 Configuration Database IRF3421 IRF3421 Configuration Database IRF3801 IRF3801 Configuration Database IRF3821 IRF3821 Configuration Database

See also the Configuration Database Overview for a comparison across products.

docs.title: AWT1000 Configuration Database
docs.text:
Complete configuration reference for AWT1000 firmware v14.2.8.

  • 200 configuration variables
  • 22 tables
  • Download JSON Schema

Using these variables

Read variables with config.get() and write them with config.set() within a configuration session. See Configuration Variables for usage examples. Table operations are documented in Tables.

docs.title: DHCP server
docs.text: Variable Default Allowed Values Description dhcp_debug disabled enabled or disabled DHCP server debugging log dhcp_endip_docker 10.0.3.253 IP address, must not be empty DOCKER DHCP ending IP address dhcp_endip_lan `` IP address, must not be empty Ending IP address LAN dhcp_endip_wan `` IP address, must not be empty Ending IP address WAN dhcp_interface_docker disabled enabled or disabled DOCKER Enable DHCP server dhcp_interface_lan disabled enabled or disabled On interface LAN dhcp_interface_wan disabled enabled or disabled On interface WAN dhcp_leasetime_docker 3600 a number 3000-700000 DOCKER DHCP lease time dhcp_leasetime_lan 3600 a number 3000-700000 DHCP lease time LAN dhcp_leasetime_wan 3600 a number 3000-700000 DHCP lease time WAN dhcp_service disabled enabled or disabled Activate DHCP server dhcp_startip_docker 10.0.3.2 IP address, must not be empty DOCKER DHCP starting IP address dhcp_startip_lan `` IP address, must not be empty Starting IP address LAN dhcp_startip_wan `` IP address, must not be empty Starting IP address WAN

docs.title: DNS
docs.text: Variable Default Allowed Values Description dhcp_sendhostname enabled enabled or disabled Register hostname on DHCP server dns1 `` IP address or empty 1st DNS server dns2 `` IP address or empty 2nd DNS server dns3 `` IP address or empty 3rd DNS server dns_domain `` DNS domain or empty Domainname (search prefix) dns_request_all disabled enabled or disabled Request names from all servers concurrently hostname AWT1000 DNS hostname, must not be empty Hostname hostname_sndyn enabled enabled or disabled Serial no. as hostname

docs.title: DNS proxy
docs.text: Variable Default Allowed Values Description dns_debug disabled enabled or disabled DNS proxy debugging log dns_proxy disabled enabled or disabled DNS Proxy dns_proxy_access `` internal interface name (maxlen 15) for which access is denied as space separated list or empty Block incoming TCP/UDP port 53 traffic on interfaces

docs.title: HTTP Proxy
docs.text: Variable Default Allowed Values Description http_proxyauth none basic ntlm or none HTTP proxy authentication http_proxyip `` IP address, DNS hostname or empty HTTP proxy IP address http_proxypass `` 0-255 printable ASCII characters (0x20-0x7E) or empty HTTP proxy password http_proxyport `` empty or a number 1-65535 HTTP proxy TCP port http_proxyuser `` 0-255 printable ASCII characters (0x20-0x7E) or empty HTTP proxy username

docs.title: IP configuration
docs.text:
Interface variables use a prefix pattern: (wan|lan|lan_port1|...|lan_port7)_<variable>.

IP Address Changes

When changing lan_ipaddr, use config_set_commit_with_ip_change() in the Python reference implementation. The device becomes unreachable at the old IP after the change. See Networking Guide.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description dns_dhcp_interface lan pseudo interface name like wan lan etc. or empty DNS via DHCP docker_ipaddr 10.0.3.1 IP address, must not be empty DOCKER IP address docker_netmask 255.255.255.0 IP address, must not be empty DOCKER Subnet mask docker_proto static Docker IP assignment protocol DOCKER IP assignment docker_snat enabled enabled or disabled SNAT on DOCKER interface gw_dhcp_interface `` pseudo interface name like wan lan etc. or empty Gateway via DHCP lan_gateway `` IP address or empty Gateway lan_ipaddr 192.168.0.254 IP address, must not be empty LAN IP address lan_netmask 255.255.255.0 IP address, must not be empty LAN Subnet mask lan_proto static static, dhcp, dhcpstatic, pppoedyn or dhcpovpn LAN IP assignment lan_snat enabled Enabled only SNAT on LAN interface lan_stp disabled enabled or disabled LAN Enable spanning tree protocol opmode transbridge transbridge only Operational mode splitbridge_docker disabled enabled or disabled put DOCKER on WAN-1 switch wan_ipaddr 192.168.1.254 IP address, must not be empty WAN IP address wan_netmask 255.255.255.0 IP address, must not be empty WAN Subnet mask wan_pppoe_password `` All printable ASCII character at least one PPPoE password wan_pppoe_userid `` OpenVPN username: must consist only of these characters: alphanumeric, underbar ("_"), dash ("-"), dot ("."), or at ("@" PPPoE username wan_pppoe_vlanid `` VLAN ID (1-4094) or empty PPPoE VLAN ID wan_proto dhcp static, dhcp, dhcpstatic, pppoedyn, monitor or disabled WAN IP assignment wan_snat enabled enabled or disabled SNAT on WAN interface

docs.title: static_routes table
docs.text:
Static routes

Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error state `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed. destination `` IP address, must not be empty IP address must be supplied or is invalid. prefix `` subnet mask as digit 8-32, optional leading / or 0 Value should be between 8-32 gateway `` IP address, must not be empty IP address must be supplied or is invalid. iface `` internal interface name or * as wildcard Invalid Interface metric `` a number 0-100 or empty Value can only be between 0-100. blx_push_state `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed.

docs.title: VPN
docs.text:
Route push is not a security boundary

Network route push (*_blxpush variables) is a routing convenience feature, not a packet filter. It controls which local networks are reachable via the VPN tunnel by advertising routes, but it does not restrict traffic. To enforce access control, use the device's packet filter or the Big-LinX server-side access policies.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description ida_scep_code `` 0-255 times: letter, digit space . _ - / or @ Big-LinX activation code ida_scep_service enabled enabled or disabled IDA SCEP Service for SW certs vpn_approval_mode disabled openvpn approval mode Approval mode for Big-LinX VPN vpn_approve_now `` 0-250 characters, excluded |;&><$()\/?=:# Do API approval for Big-LinX VPN vpn_ida_enable_on_alarm false true or false Enable Big-LinX VPN connections on ALARM vpn_ida_enable_on_cut true true or false Enable Big-LinX VPN connections on VPNKEY vpn_ida_enb_on_alarm_type enabled enabled or disabled Behavior of Big-LinX VPN connections on ALARM vpn_ida_inactivity_timeout 0 only digits, must not be empty Big-LinX VPN inactivity timeout vpn_ida_loglevel info none info debug or verbose Big-LinX log level vpn_ida_proxy disabled enabled or disabled Use HTTP/HTTPS proxy for Big-LinX vpn_ida_slow_link disabled enabled or disabled BLX VPN slow link vpn_ida_snat `` IP address with subnetmnask /0-32 or empty SNAT for destination wwh_service enabled enabled or disabled activate world-wide heartbeat wwh_service_wwh3_protocol disabled enabled or disabled activate protocol WWH3 for wwh-service

docs.title: Big-LinX IoT push
docs.text: Variable Default Allowed Values Description blxpush_service disabled enabled or disabled activate IOT data-push datacollectpushd_push_cycle_time 10000 1000-9999999 Transmission cycle time (ms)

docs.title: permissions table
docs.text:
Configuration Permissions

Controls per-user read/write access to configuration variables and tables. configid values: 0 = default permissions, 1-9999 = config variable ID, table name (e.g., "macgroups") = table access.

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
# Give user "operator" write access to config variable #42
dev.table_insert("permissions", cfg_session_id, ["operator", "42", "w"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error username `` 4-16 characters out of letters digits or - 4-16 characters or digits must be used configid `` 1-255 digits or letters or underline, must not be empty Only numbers, characters and underscores are allowed permission `` r or w Valid values are "r" or "w" Factory defaults (37 rows) | `username` | `configid` | `permission` | |---|---|---| | admin | 0 | w | | guest | 0 | r | | Big-LinX | 0 | r | | Big-LinX | 262 | w | | Big-LinX | 2237 | w | | Big-LinX | 2238 | w | | Big-LinX | 3460 | w | | Big-LinX | 3459 | w | | Big-LinX | 2239 | w | | Big-LinX | 3443 | w | | Big-LinX | 238 | w | | Big-LinX | 3742 | w | | Big-LinX | 3951 | w | | Big-LinX | 3954 | w | | Big-LinX | 3956 | w | | Big-LinX | 3957 | w | | Big-LinX | 3781 | w | | Big-LinX | 3770 | w | | Big-LinX | 3769 | w | | Big-LinX | 3768 | w | | Big-LinX | 3767 | w | | Big-LinX | 3766 | w | | Big-LinX | 3765 | w | | Big-LinX | 3764 | w | | Big-LinX | 3763 | w | | Big-LinX | 3894 | w | | Big-LinX | 3895 | w | | Big-LinX | 3896 | w | | Big-LinX | 4042 | w | | Big-LinX | 2227 | w | | Big-LinX | 3779 | w | | Big-LinX | 132 | w | | Big-LinX | 3514 | w | | Big-LinX | 3714 | w | | Big-LinX | 5263 | w | | Big-LinX | 5264 | w | | Big-LinX | 5265 | w |

docs.title: users table
docs.text:
Configuration User Accounts

Setting the password

After inserting a user row, set the password in the same configuration session:

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("users", cfg_session_id,
    ["testuser", "", "", "1", "", "1970-01-01", "1970-01-01"])
dev.config_set(cfg_session_id, {
    "webpwd_user": "testuser",
    "webpwd_user_new": "SecurePassword123!",
    "webpwd_user_checked": "SecurePassword123!"
})
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 4-16 characters out of letters digits or - 4-16 characters or digits must be used password_des `` DES Hash Only DES hash is allowed password_md5 `` MD5 Hash Only MD5 hash is allowed enabled `` 0 or 1 Only the values 0 or 1 are allowed. password_argon2 `` argon2 hash or empty Only argon2 hash is allowed password_changetime `` Date in YYYY-MM-DD Format Date only allowed in the format YYYY-MM-DD password_lifetime `` Date in YYYY-MM-DD Format Date only allowed in the format YYYY-MM-DD

docs.title: Software update
docs.text:
# Trigger factory reset (irreversible!)
try:
    dev.config_set_commit({"fw_restore_now": "1"})
except Exception:
    pass  # Expected — device reboots and closes the connection
jsonrpcdevice.wait_for_host_is_online("192.168.0.254", timeout=300, interval=5)

Factory Reset

Setting fw_restore_now is irreversible. All configuration is lost and the device returns to factory defaults, including the default IP address and credentials.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description update_filename `` filename with .bin Filename and location update_mode remotefirmware remotefirmware or localfirmware, not empty Update mode update_proto http protocol: tftp, ftp, http or https Update protocol update_restoredefaults disabled enabled or disabled Set the factory defaults of the new firmware update_server `` IP address or hostname with optional :port Server address

docs.title: IRF1401 Configuration Database
docs.text:
Complete configuration reference for IRF1401 firmware v2.2.8.

  • 709 configuration variables
  • 34 tables
  • Download JSON Schema

Using these variables

Read variables with config.get() and write them with config.set() within a configuration session. See Configuration Variables for usage examples. Table operations are documented in Tables.

docs.title: IP configuration
docs.text:
Interface variables use a prefix pattern: (wan|lan|lan_port1|...|lan_port7)_<variable>.

IP Address Changes

When changing lan_ipaddr, use config_set_commit_with_ip_change() in the Python reference implementation. The device becomes unreachable at the old IP after the change. See Networking Guide.

docs.title: 1:1 NAT
docs.text:
1:1 NAT translates entire subnets by replacing the network prefix while preserving the host portion. This is used to resolve IP address conflicts when two networks share the same address range. Variables use the interface prefix pattern: (wan|lan|lan_port[1-7])_.

The public (virtual) subnet is derived from the interface's _ipaddr and _netmask. Double-sided network mapping (_netmap_ext_*) controls the virtual address range under which the opposite side's devices appear.

DHCP interfaces

On interfaces using dhcp or dhcpovpn protocol, the private subnet is derived automatically from the DHCP lease. The _netmap_subnet variable is only used for static interfaces.

See the 1:1 NAT guide for a complete walkthrough.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description docker_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled DOCKER Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping docker_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed DOCKER Substitution subnet for private IPs docker_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled DOCKER Enable 1:1 NAT docker_netmap_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed DOCKER Mapped 1:1 NAT subnet l3tap10_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled BLX-VPN Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping l3tap10_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed BLX-VPN Substitution subnet for private IPs l3tap10_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled BLX-VPN Enable 1:1 NAT lan_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled LAN Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping lan_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed LAN Substitution subnet for private IPs lan_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled LAN Enable 1:1 NAT lan_netmap_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed LAN Mapped 1:1 NAT subnet wan_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled WAN Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping wan_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed WAN Substitution subnet for private IPs wan_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled WAN Enable 1:1 NAT wan_netmap_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed WAN Mapped 1:1 NAT subnet

docs.title: forwarding table
docs.text:
Configuration of IP fowarding

Defines DNAT (port forwarding) rules. Changes take effect immediately on commit — no trigger variable is needed (unlike packet filter tables which require writing to l3_forward).

wan_ifname = dev.config_get(["wan_ifname"])["result"][0]["wan_ifname"]
cfg = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("forwarding", cfg, [
    wan_ifname, "tcp", "", "3389", "192.168.10.200",
    "3389", "disabled", "", "RDP access", "enabled", "1", "disabled",
])
dev.sess_commit(cfg)

See the Port Forwarding guide for SNAT usage and more examples.

Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error fwifname `` internal network interface name (maxlen 15) or empty Invalid Interface name fwproto `` tcp udp or * Field can only contain the values tcp, udp or * fwlocalip `` IP address or empty IP adress is invalid fwlocalport `` port from 1 to 65535 or port range like 1-65535. Range uses dash with start-end or empty Value can only be between 1-65535 or defining a range fwtargetip `` IP address, must not be empty IP address must be supplied or is invalid. fwtargetport `` port from 1 to 65535 or port range like 1-65535. Range uses dash with start-end or empty Value can only be between 1-65535 or defining a range fwsnat `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed. fwsrcnet `` IP address with subnetmnask /0-32 or empty IP address or subnet mask invalid. Subnet mask must be separated by / and may range between 1 and 32. fwcomment `` ascii character range Z to ^ or empty Field contains unsupported characters fwenabled `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed. fwrsnat `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed.

docs.title: macgroups table
docs.text:
Configuration Hardware Groups

Used for grouping devices by MAC address (e.g., for L2 packet filter rules with @groupname syntax).

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("macgroups", cfg_session_id, ["printers", "00:10:20:45:67:89"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 1-20 letters, digits or . - _ @ / # but no space The field must not be empty and can contain up to 20 valid characters hwaddr `` MAC address with : as delimiter or * but not empty Invalid MAC address.

docs.title: ipgroups table
docs.text:
Configuration Network Groups

Used for grouping IP subnets (e.g., for packet filter rules with @groupname syntax).

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("ipgroups", cfg_session_id, ["office", "192.168.1.0/24"])
dev.table_insert("ipgroups", cfg_session_id, ["office", "10.0.0.0/8"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 1-20 letters, digits or . - _ @ / # but no space The field must not be empty and can contain up to 20 valid characters network `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty Value is invalid. A DNS domain or DNS hostname or alternatively an IP address with network mask is required, this must be separated with a / and can be between 1 and 32.

docs.title: Packet filter
docs.text:
Reload required after table changes

Modifying the packet filter tables (services, serv_Protocols, selected_services) does not automatically reload the running filter. You must write to l3_forward (for L3 rules) or l2_forward (for L2 rules) to apply changes.

docs.title: serv_Protocols table
docs.text:
API: Packet filter rules

Individual filter rules within a packet filter ruleset. Each rule specifies match criteria (protocol, addresses, ports) and an action. Action IDs: 1=ACCEPT, 2=DROP, 5=CUT, 6=REJECT, 7=INACTIVE, 8=CUTANDACCEPT.

See the Packet Filter guide for action IDs, rule modes, and examples.

docs.title: services table
docs.text:
API: Rulesets for packet filter

Integer id columns require explicit values

Tables with an integer id primary key (services, serv_Protocols, selected_services) do not support auto-increment. Passing "" or "0" for the id field causes a "datatype mismatch" error at commit. Always provide a unique non-zero integer.

Custom entries must use IDs starting at 1000 or higher. IDs below 1000 are reserved for factory defaults.

See the Packet Filter guide for factory defaults, interface names, and examples.

docs.title: Big-LinX configuration
docs.text:
Route push is not a security boundary

Network route push (*_blxpush variables) is a routing convenience feature, not a packet filter. It controls which local networks are reachable via the VPN tunnel by advertising routes, but it does not restrict traffic. To enforce access control, use the device's packet filter or the Big-LinX server-side access policies.

docs.title: permissions table
docs.text:
Configuration Permissions

Controls per-user read/write access to configuration variables and tables. configid values: 0 = default permissions, 1-9999 = config variable ID, table name (e.g., "macgroups") = table access.

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
# Give user "operator" write access to config variable #42
dev.table_insert("permissions", cfg_session_id, ["operator", "42", "w"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error username `` 4-16 characters out of letters digits or - 4-16 characters or digits must be used configid `` 1-255 digits or letters or underline, must not be empty Only numbers, characters and underscores are allowed permission `` r or w Valid values are "r" or "w" Factory defaults (37 rows) | `username` | `configid` | `permission` | |---|---|---| | admin | 0 | w | | guest | 0 | r | | Big-LinX | 0 | r | | Big-LinX | 262 | w | | Big-LinX | 2237 | w | | Big-LinX | 2238 | w | | Big-LinX | 3460 | w | | Big-LinX | 3459 | w | | Big-LinX | 2239 | w | | Big-LinX | 3443 | w | | Big-LinX | 238 | w | | Big-LinX | 3742 | w | | Big-LinX | 3951 | w | | Big-LinX | 3954 | w | | Big-LinX | 3956 | w | | Big-LinX | 3957 | w | | Big-LinX | 3781 | w | | Big-LinX | 3770 | w | | Big-LinX | 3769 | w | | Big-LinX | 3768 | w | | Big-LinX | 3767 | w | | Big-LinX | 3766 | w | | Big-LinX | 3765 | w | | Big-LinX | 3764 | w | | Big-LinX | 3763 | w | | Big-LinX | 3894 | w | | Big-LinX | 3895 | w | | Big-LinX | 3896 | w | | Big-LinX | 4042 | w | | Big-LinX | 2227 | w | | Big-LinX | 3779 | w | | Big-LinX | 132 | w | | Big-LinX | 3514 | w | | Big-LinX | 3714 | w | | Big-LinX | 5263 | w | | Big-LinX | 5264 | w | | Big-LinX | 5265 | w |

docs.title: users table
docs.text:
Configuration User Accounts

Setting the password

After inserting a user row, set the password in the same configuration session:

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("users", cfg_session_id,
    ["testuser", "", "", "1", "", "1970-01-01", "1970-01-01"])
dev.config_set(cfg_session_id, {
    "webpwd_user": "testuser",
    "webpwd_user_new": "SecurePassword123!",
    "webpwd_user_checked": "SecurePassword123!"
})
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 4-16 characters out of letters digits or - 4-16 characters or digits must be used password_des `` DES Hash Only DES hash is allowed password_md5 `` MD5 Hash Only MD5 hash is allowed enabled `` 0 or 1 Only the values 0 or 1 are allowed. password_argon2 `` argon2 hash or empty Only argon2 hash is allowed password_changetime `` Date in YYYY-MM-DD Format Date only allowed in the format YYYY-MM-DD password_lifetime `` Date in YYYY-MM-DD Format Date only allowed in the format YYYY-MM-DD

Factory defaults (3 rows):

docs.title: Software update
docs.text:
# Trigger factory reset (irreversible!)
try:
    dev.config_set_commit({"fw_restore_now": "1"})
except Exception:
    pass  # Expected — device reboots and closes the connection
jsonrpcdevice.wait_for_host_is_online("192.168.0.254", timeout=300, interval=5)

Factory Reset

Setting fw_restore_now is irreversible. All configuration is lost and the device returns to factory defaults, including the default IP address and credentials.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description update_filename `` filename with .bin Filename and location update_mode remotefirmware remotefirmware or localfirmware, not empty Update mode update_proto http protocol: tftp, ftp, http or https Update protocol update_restoredefaults disabled enabled or disabled Set the factory defaults of the new firmware update_server `` IP address or hostname with optional :port Server address

docs.title: IRF1421 Configuration Database
docs.text:
Complete configuration reference for IRF1421 firmware v2.2.8.

  • 751 configuration variables
  • 34 tables
  • Download JSON Schema

Using these variables

Read variables with config.get() and write them with config.set() within a configuration session. See Configuration Variables for usage examples. Table operations are documented in Tables.

docs.title: IP configuration
docs.text:
Interface variables use a prefix pattern: (wan|lan|lan_port1|...|lan_port7)_<variable>.

IP Address Changes

When changing lan_ipaddr, use config_set_commit_with_ip_change() in the Python reference implementation. The device becomes unreachable at the old IP after the change. See Networking Guide.

docs.title: 1:1 NAT
docs.text:
1:1 NAT translates entire subnets by replacing the network prefix while preserving the host portion. This is used to resolve IP address conflicts when two networks share the same address range. Variables use the interface prefix pattern: (wan|lan|lan_port[1-7])_.

The public (virtual) subnet is derived from the interface's _ipaddr and _netmask. Double-sided network mapping (_netmap_ext_*) controls the virtual address range under which the opposite side's devices appear.

DHCP interfaces

On interfaces using dhcp or dhcpovpn protocol, the private subnet is derived automatically from the DHCP lease. The _netmap_subnet variable is only used for static interfaces.

See the 1:1 NAT guide for a complete walkthrough.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description docker_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled DOCKER Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping docker_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed DOCKER Substitution subnet for private IPs docker_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled DOCKER Enable 1:1 NAT docker_netmap_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed DOCKER Mapped 1:1 NAT subnet l3tap10_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled BLX-VPN Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping l3tap10_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed BLX-VPN Substitution subnet for private IPs l3tap10_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled BLX-VPN Enable 1:1 NAT lan_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled LAN Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping lan_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed LAN Substitution subnet for private IPs lan_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled LAN Enable 1:1 NAT lan_netmap_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed LAN Mapped 1:1 NAT subnet wan_netmap_ext_status disabled enabled or disabled WAN Enable/Disable Double Sided Network Mapping wan_netmap_ext_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed WAN Substitution subnet for private IPs wan_netmap_status disabled enabled or disabled WAN Enable 1:1 NAT wan_netmap_subnet `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty and /32 is not allowed WAN Mapped 1:1 NAT subnet

docs.title: forwarding table
docs.text:
Configuration of IP fowarding

Defines DNAT (port forwarding) rules. Changes take effect immediately on commit — no trigger variable is needed (unlike packet filter tables which require writing to l3_forward).

wan_ifname = dev.config_get(["wan_ifname"])["result"][0]["wan_ifname"]
cfg = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("forwarding", cfg, [
    wan_ifname, "tcp", "", "3389", "192.168.10.200",
    "3389", "disabled", "", "RDP access", "enabled", "1", "disabled",
])
dev.sess_commit(cfg)

See the Port Forwarding guide for SNAT usage and more examples.

Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error fwifname `` internal network interface name (maxlen 15) or empty Invalid Interface name fwproto `` tcp udp or * Field can only contain the values tcp, udp or * fwlocalip `` IP address or empty IP adress is invalid fwlocalport `` port from 1 to 65535 or port range like 1-65535. Range uses dash with start-end or empty Value can only be between 1-65535 or defining a range fwtargetip `` IP address, must not be empty IP address must be supplied or is invalid. fwtargetport `` port from 1 to 65535 or port range like 1-65535. Range uses dash with start-end or empty Value can only be between 1-65535 or defining a range fwsnat `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed. fwsrcnet `` IP address with subnetmnask /0-32 or empty IP address or subnet mask invalid. Subnet mask must be separated by / and may range between 1 and 32. fwcomment `` ascii character range Z to ^ or empty Field contains unsupported characters fwenabled `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed. fwrsnat `` enabled or disabled Only the values enabled or disabled are allowed.

docs.title: macgroups table
docs.text:
Configuration Hardware Groups

Used for grouping devices by MAC address (e.g., for L2 packet filter rules with @groupname syntax).

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("macgroups", cfg_session_id, ["printers", "00:10:20:45:67:89"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 1-20 letters, digits or . - _ @ / # but no space The field must not be empty and can contain up to 20 valid characters hwaddr `` MAC address with : as delimiter or * but not empty Invalid MAC address.

docs.title: ipgroups table
docs.text:
Configuration Network Groups

Used for grouping IP subnets (e.g., for packet filter rules with @groupname syntax).

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("ipgroups", cfg_session_id, ["office", "192.168.1.0/24"])
dev.table_insert("ipgroups", cfg_session_id, ["office", "10.0.0.0/8"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 1-20 letters, digits or . - _ @ / # but no space The field must not be empty and can contain up to 20 valid characters network `` IP address with subnet mask i.e. 192.168.0.0/24, must not be empty Value is invalid. A DNS domain or DNS hostname or alternatively an IP address with network mask is required, this must be separated with a / and can be between 1 and 32.

docs.title: Packet filter
docs.text:
Reload required after table changes

Modifying the packet filter tables (services, serv_Protocols, selected_services) does not automatically reload the running filter. You must write to l3_forward (for L3 rules) or l2_forward (for L2 rules) to apply changes.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description l2_forward `` 0-64 times: letter, digit . _ - Apply layer 2 packet filter configuration (trigger variable, triggered with Unix timestamp) l3_forward `` 0-64 times: letter, digit . _ - Apply layer 3 packet filter configuration (trigger variable, triggered with Unix timestamp)

docs.title: serv_Protocols table
docs.text:
API: Packet filter rules

Individual filter rules within a packet filter ruleset. Each rule specifies match criteria (protocol, addresses, ports) and an action. Action IDs: 1=ACCEPT, 2=DROP, 5=CUT, 6=REJECT, 7=INACTIVE, 8=CUTANDACCEPT.

See the Packet Filter guide for action IDs, rule modes, and examples.

docs.title: services table
docs.text:
API: Rulesets for packet filter

Integer id columns require explicit values

Tables with an integer id primary key (services, serv_Protocols, selected_services) do not support auto-increment. Passing "" or "0" for the id field causes a "datatype mismatch" error at commit. Always provide a unique non-zero integer.

Custom entries must use IDs starting at 1000 or higher. IDs below 1000 are reserved for factory defaults.

See the Packet Filter guide for factory defaults, interface names, and examples.

Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error id `` empty or a number 1-65535 Value can only be between 1-65535 name `` ruleset name Error: Only these characters are allowed: -_a-zA-Z0-9 maximum of 32 characters description `` ruleset name Error: Only these characters are allowed: ,.-_a-zA-Z0-9 or space, maximum of 255 characters layer_id `` 1 or 2 not empty Only the values 1 or 2 are allowed. def_serv `` o or 1 not empty The value is invalid or contains invalid characters src_interf_name `` internal interface name with ! as prefix or * as wildcard Error: Only valid interface name with optional prefix ! or * allowed des_interf_name `` internal interface name with ! as prefix or * as wildcard Error: Only valid interface name with optional prefix ! or * allowed fac_def_pos `` a number 0-65535 or empty Value can only be between 0-65535 or empty Factory defaults (9 rows) | `id` | `name` | `description` | `layer_id` | `def_serv` | `src_interf_name` | `des_interf_name` | `fac_def_pos` | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | ARP | ARP address resolution | 1 | 1 | * | * | 1 | | 2 | ICMP_L2 | Allow all Layer 2 ICMP packets | 1 | 1 | * | * | None | | 3 | ICMP_L3 | Allow all Layer 3 ICMP packets | 2 | 1 | * | * | None | | 4 | Allow_L2 | Allow all L2 traffic | 1 | 1 | * | * | 2 | | 5 | Allow_L3 | Allow all L3 traffic | 2 | 1 | * | * | 3 | | 6 | Block_L2 | Block all L2 traffic | 1 | 1 | * | * | None | | 7 | Block_L3 | Block all L3 traffic | 2 | 1 | * | * | None | | 8 | Log_L2 | Log and block all packets. | 1 | 1 | * | * | None | | 999 | Log_L3 | Log and block all packets. | 2 | 1 | * | * | None |

docs.title: Big-LinX configuration
docs.text:
Route push is not a security boundary

Network route push (*_blxpush variables) is a routing convenience feature, not a packet filter. It controls which local networks are reachable via the VPN tunnel by advertising routes, but it does not restrict traffic. To enforce access control, use the device's packet filter or the Big-LinX server-side access policies.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description ida_scep_code `` 0-255 times: letter, digit space . _ - / or @ Big-LinX activation code ida_scep_service disabled enabled or disabled IDA SCEP Service for SW certs sc_pin 9999 4 digits Smartcard PIN vpn_approval_mode disabled openvpn approval mode Approval mode for Big-LinX VPN vpn_approve_now `` 0-250 characters, excluded |;&><$()\/?=:# Do API approval for Big-LinX VPN vpn_ida_enable_on_alarm false true or false Enable Big-LinX VPN connections on ALARM vpn_ida_enable_on_cut true true or false Enable Big-LinX VPN connections on VPNKEY vpn_ida_enb_on_alarm_type enabled enabled or disabled Behavior of Big-LinX VPN connections on ALARM vpn_ida_enb_on_cut_type enabled enabled or disabled Behavior of Big-LinX VPN connections on VPNKEY vpn_ida_inactivity_timeout 0 only digits, must not be empty Big-LinX VPN inactivity timeout vpn_ida_loglevel info none info debug or verbose Big-LinX log level vpn_ida_proxy disabled enabled or disabled Use HTTP/HTTPS proxy for Big-LinX vpn_ida_slow_link disabled enabled or disabled BLX VPN slow link vpn_ida_snat `` IP address with subnetmnask /0-32 or empty SNAT for destination wwh_service enabled enabled or disabled activate world-wide heartbeat wwh_service_wwh3_protocol disabled enabled or disabled activate protocol WWH3 for wwh-service

docs.title: permissions table
docs.text:
Configuration Permissions

Controls per-user read/write access to configuration variables and tables. configid values: 0 = default permissions, 1-9999 = config variable ID, table name (e.g., "macgroups") = table access.

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
# Give user "operator" write access to config variable #42
dev.table_insert("permissions", cfg_session_id, ["operator", "42", "w"])
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error username `` 4-16 characters out of letters digits or - 4-16 characters or digits must be used configid `` 1-255 digits or letters or underline, must not be empty Only numbers, characters and underscores are allowed permission `` r or w Valid values are "r" or "w" Factory defaults (37 rows) | `username` | `configid` | `permission` | |---|---|---| | admin | 0 | w | | guest | 0 | r | | Big-LinX | 0 | r | | Big-LinX | 262 | w | | Big-LinX | 2237 | w | | Big-LinX | 2238 | w | | Big-LinX | 3460 | w | | Big-LinX | 3459 | w | | Big-LinX | 2239 | w | | Big-LinX | 3443 | w | | Big-LinX | 238 | w | | Big-LinX | 3742 | w | | Big-LinX | 3951 | w | | Big-LinX | 3954 | w | | Big-LinX | 3956 | w | | Big-LinX | 3957 | w | | Big-LinX | 3781 | w | | Big-LinX | 3770 | w | | Big-LinX | 3769 | w | | Big-LinX | 3768 | w | | Big-LinX | 3767 | w | | Big-LinX | 3766 | w | | Big-LinX | 3765 | w | | Big-LinX | 3764 | w | | Big-LinX | 3763 | w | | Big-LinX | 3894 | w | | Big-LinX | 3895 | w | | Big-LinX | 3896 | w | | Big-LinX | 4042 | w | | Big-LinX | 2227 | w | | Big-LinX | 3779 | w | | Big-LinX | 132 | w | | Big-LinX | 3514 | w | | Big-LinX | 3714 | w | | Big-LinX | 5263 | w | | Big-LinX | 5264 | w | | Big-LinX | 5265 | w |

docs.title: users table
docs.text:
Configuration User Accounts

Setting the password

After inserting a user row, set the password in the same configuration session:

cfg_session_id = dev.sess_start()
dev.table_insert("users", cfg_session_id,
    ["testuser", "", "", "1", "", "1970-01-01", "1970-01-01"])
dev.config_set(cfg_session_id, {
    "webpwd_user": "testuser",
    "webpwd_user_new": "SecurePassword123!",
    "webpwd_user_checked": "SecurePassword123!"
})
dev.sess_commit(cfg_session_id)
Column Default Allowed Values Validation Error name `` 4-16 characters out of letters digits or - 4-16 characters or digits must be used password_des `` DES Hash Only DES hash is allowed password_md5 `` MD5 Hash Only MD5 hash is allowed enabled `` 0 or 1 Only the values 0 or 1 are allowed. password_argon2 `` argon2 hash or empty Only argon2 hash is allowed password_changetime `` Date in YYYY-MM-DD Format Date only allowed in the format YYYY-MM-DD password_lifetime `` Date in YYYY-MM-DD Format Date only allowed in the format YYYY-MM-DD

docs.title: Software update
docs.text:
# Trigger factory reset (irreversible!)
try:
    dev.config_set_commit({"fw_restore_now": "1"})
except Exception:
    pass  # Expected — device reboots and closes the connection
jsonrpcdevice.wait_for_host_is_online("192.168.0.254", timeout=300, interval=5)

Factory Reset

Setting fw_restore_now is irreversible. All configuration is lost and the device returns to factory defaults, including the default IP address and credentials.

Variable Default Allowed Values Description update_filename `` filename with .bin Filename and location update_mode remotefirmware remotefirmware or localfirmware, not empty Update mode update_proto http protocol: tftp, ftp, http or https Update protocol update_restoredefaults disabled enabled or disabled Set the factory defaults of the new firmware update_server `` IP address or hostname with optional :port Server address

docs.title: IRF3401 Configuration Database
docs.text:
Complete configuration reference for IRF3401 firmware v2.2.8.

  • 763 configuration variables
  • 35 tables
  • Download JSON Schema

Using these variables

Read variables with config.get() and write them with config.set() within a configuration session. See Configuration Variables for usage examples. Table operations are documented in Tables.
